info.name: Ginja Console — Platform API
info.description: Generated from controller classes (verified). 80 endpoints across 15 controllers.

request.description: Generate 10 single-use MFA recovery codes (bearer). Returns plaintext ONCE; invalidates previous set.
Remaining unused backup codes (count only; codes never returned again).

item.name: Dashboard
item.description: Console home — one aggregate read (KPIs, tenant status, onboarding funnel, approvals, recent activity, health).

request.description: Aggregated console overview. Roles: PLATFORM_ADMIN/SUPPORT/PLATFORM_APPROVER/PLATFORM_ENGINEER.
Clear a member's failed-login lockout so they can sign in again. PLATFORM_ADMIN.

item.name: My Account

request.description: The current member's profile. Any authenticated member.
Partial self-profile update. Email is NOT editable (admin-managed).
Multipart JPG/PNG. Stored via the document service; returns the refreshed profile with a fresh avatar_url.
Remove my profile photo.
My roles & access (view only): role, description, granted_by, granted_at, scopes[].
ok | soon | expired | pending, with days_left / expires_at (90-day rotation).
Active password rules for the change-password checklist.
Verify current password, set new (complexity + no-reuse enforced), sign out all other sessions.
{ mandatory, allowed:{totp,sms,email,webauthn}, enabled:{...}, backup_codes_remaining }.
Emails a 6-digit code; returns { expires_at }.
Confirm email-OTP enrolment with the emailed code.
Disable one MFA method (totp|sms|email|webauthn). Blocked if MFA is mandatory and it's your last active method.
My active sessions: browser/os/device/ip/last_seen/current.
Sign out one of my OTHER sessions (400 if it's the current one — use logout).
Sign out all my other devices → { revoked: n }.
My recent security events (read-only, newest first, capped 50).

item.description: Personal account management (self-service). Base /platform/organization/me — every request acts on the caller (token), no member-id param. Tabs: Profile, Password, MFA, Active sessions, Security activity, My roles & access.

request.description: Replace a role's data-residency region scopes (empty/omitted = no restriction). Regions come from /platform/settings/data-residency.

body.raw:
{
  "code": "REPORTS_EXPORT",
  "name": "Export Reports",
  "description": "Allows exporting directories, logs and reports",
  "group_code": "OBSERVABILITY",
  "group_label": "Observability",
  "sensitive": false
}

{
  "code": "DEDICATED",
  "name": "Dedicated database",
  "description": "Tenant gets its own database.",
  "enabled": true,
  "sort_order": 4
}

request.description: All active sessions across users, GROUPED BY MEMBER (admin/support). result is an array of MemberSessionsResponse: { member_id, account, user, session_count, sessions[] }, members ordered by most-recent activity. Each sessions[] entry is enriched with browser/os/device + current flag. Per-user 'revoke all' → revoke-all?member_id={member_id}.
Revoke a single session by id (PLATFORM_ADMIN).
Revoke all active sessions of a member (PLATFORM_ADMIN). Returns {revoked:n}.

List the runtime-editable notification type→template mappings (MEMBER_INVITE, TENANT_ADMIN_INVITE, SMS_OTP). Each: { type, channel, template_id, template_code, description, active, updated_by, ... }. ADMIN/SUPPORT.

Set a notification type's template_id / template_code / active at runtime (partial; only non-null fields applied). Takes effect on the NEXT send — no redeploy. {type} ∈ MEMBER_INVITE | TENANT_ADMIN_INVITE | SMS_OTP. active:false = per-type kill switch. ADMIN. Audited NOTIFICATION_TEMPLATE_UPDATED.
MFA adoption summary + per-user enrolment (admin/support). methods=totp/sms/webauthn; backup_codes/enabled_on null (not modeled).
One user's MFA detail (admin/support).
Send an MFA-enrolment reminder to a user (PLATFORM_ADMIN). Audit-logged.

query.description: Search by member name or email (optional).

request.description: Password-expiry summary + paged, filterable per-user status (admin/support). Each PwdUser adds account_status, roles, activity and MFA flags. Password status=ok/soon/expired/pending from passwordChangedAt + policy. summary always reflects the full population; users is a paged envelope (content + page/size/total_elements/total_pages).
One user's password status (admin/support).
Force a password reset (PLATFORM_ADMIN): revokes sessions + reset notification. Audit-logged.
Platform-default locale/formatting (admin/support).
Partial update of localization settings (admin).
Current published validation ruleset + grouped rules (admin/support).
Ruleset version history (admin/support).
Create/return an editable DRAFT cloning current rules (admin).
Save the draft's grouped rules (admin).
Publish the draft (archives prior published) (admin).
Restore a prior version as a new published version (admin).
Live-test a value against a rule pattern + normalize steps (admin/support).

query.description: When editing, set to the module's own business id so its current value isn't flagged.

request.description: Real-time duplicate check for module code and/or name (use on the create/edit form before submit). Supply either or both of code/name. code is checked in normalised UPPER_SNAKE form; name is case-insensitive. Returns { code, name } each as { value, normalized, available, reason } (null if not supplied). Mirrors the server guards — register/update reject duplicates with 409.
Version history for the module, newest first. One PUBLISHED (current=true), the rest ARCHIVED. Each row carries the change note, the snapshot, and timestamps.

Diffs two versions: both snapshots plus sub_modules_added / sub_modules_removed and changed_fields — an array of { field, from, to } (one entry per differing field: name, description, owner_team, status, note).

Restores the given version's snapshot onto the live module (fields + sub-modules) and publishes it as a NEW current version (auto-incremented minor). The prior current version is archived; nothing is overwritten. Returns the refreshed ModuleResponse.
Stream the filtered audit trail as CSV (default) or JSON (?format=json). Roles: PLATFORM_ADMIN/SUPPORT.

Create a DRAFT pricing structure. Each component takes an optional label (<=120 chars) - the human-readable rate-card tab name (e.g. "Outpatient claim", "Inpatient claim"), distinct from the machine component_type; the UI falls back to the type when omitted.
Minimal ACTIVE pricing structures for the onboarding subscription-model picker (no components/tiers). PLATFORM_ADMIN.
List payers with optional filters (status, payer_type, from_date/to_date), sorting and pagination. Returns a paged envelope {content,page,size,total_elements,total_pages}. PLATFORM_ADMIN.

formdata.description: Leave disabled to CREATE. To REPLACE an existing document's file, enable this and set it to that document's file_id (category is then ignored).

request.description: Multipart upload. The `file` part carries the bytes (ops proxies them to ginja-ai-document-service); `category` is required when creating, `description`/`expiry_date` optional. To REPLACE the file behind an existing document (e.g. wrong file uploaded), enable the `file_id` part and set it to that document's file_id — ops stores the bytes as a NEW VERSION of that same file_id (file_id unchanged, version_number increments, prior version retained in the document service's history; keeps document_id + category, status resets to PENDING_REVIEW). Select a file for the `file` part before sending. See the dedicated 'replace document file' request below.

formdata.description: The existing document's document-service file_id to replace. Must belong to this tenant.

request.description: REPLACE the file behind an already-uploaded document (same endpoint as upload). Pass the existing `file_id` + the new `file`; no `category` needed (the document keeps its category and document_id, status resets to PENDING_REVIEW). Ops stores the bytes as a NEW VERSION against the same file_id via the document service's POST /document/{fileId}/version — file_id is unchanged, version_number increments, and the prior version is retained in the document service's history (GET /document/{fileId}/versions). Errors: 404 unknown file_id; 400 if file_id belongs to another tenant or a supplied category mismatches. Audited as DOCUMENT_REPLACED.

Fetches a tenant document's metadata plus a freshly issued, time-limited pre-signed download URL from ginja-ai-document-service. `documentId` is the business id (DOC...) and must belong to the tenant under the payer. Returns 404 if the payer, tenant, or document is unknown.
Partial update of a tenant on a DRAFT payer (only sent fields applied). 409 if not DRAFT / dup subdomain or (legal,country). Returns PayerResponse.
Delete a SECONDARY tenant on a DRAFT payer. 409 if not DRAFT or deleting the primary. Returns refreshed PayerResponse.

query.description: optional: filter to a teammate's drafts

request.description: 'Onboarding drafts in progress' directory panel. Returns a LIST where each item is the same OnboardingProgressResponse as GET /platform/payers/{payerId}/steps (completed/total/progress_pct/completed_steps/incomplete_steps/steps[]). Optional ?assignee= for 'By teammate'. PLATFORM_ADMIN.

item.name: Payer Approval

request.description: Final approve \u2192 auto-activate (PLATFORM_APPROVER, comment optional). 409 unless awaiting approval AND provisioning complete (every tenant READY_TO_ACTIVATE) AND all four review sections APPROVED (per-section checklist, see the sections/{sectionKey} requests; the error lists outstanding section keys). 403 if the caller submitted this payer (SoD). Watch all_sections_approved on GET /platform/approvals/{payerId} to know when this will pass.

Approve one review section of a submitted payer (PLATFORM_APPROVER). sectionKey \u2208 primary_tenant_details | module_entitlements | subscription_billing | kyb_documents. comment optional. Upserts the section state; returns the refreshed ApprovalReviewResponse (its sections[] now carry review_status/decided_by/comment/decided_at). Does not require provisioning complete; SoD enforced (submitter cannot decide).
Reject one review section (PLATFORM_APPROVER). comment is mandatory (400 if missing). Returns the refreshed ApprovalReviewResponse.
Flag one review section as needing more information (PLATFORM_APPROVER). comment is mandatory (400 if missing). Returns the refreshed ApprovalReviewResponse.

item.description: Final maker-checker gate. Only payers whose every tenant is READY_TO_ACTIVATE (provisioning complete, §Provisioning) appear in the queue / can be approved; approving earlier returns 409. Approve auto-activates (go-live); submitter != approver is enforced (403).

item.name: Approval Queue

request.description: Maker-checker approval queue (lean rows). ?status=pending|approved|all. PLATFORM_APPROVER.
Full review payload for one request: header + review meta (status, can_decide, sections, auto_activate_note) + full payer aggregate. PLATFORM_APPROVER.

item.description: Final maker-checker gate. Only payers whose every tenant is READY_TO_ACTIVATE (provisioning complete, §Provisioning) appear in the queue / can be approved; approving earlier returns 409. Approve auto-activates (go-live); submitter != approver is enforced (403).

request.description: MAKER step (maker-checker). Raises a PENDING SUSPEND request — does NOT suspend immediately. Returns a LifecycleChangeRequestResponse (request_id LCR…, status PENDING). Payer must be ACTIVE with no request already pending (else 409). A checker with APPROVAL_DECIDE must approve it (see the approve request below).
MAKER step. Raises a PENDING REACTIVATE request for a SUSPENDED payer (optional comment). A checker must approve to actually reactivate.
MAKER step. Raises a PENDING RETIRE request for a SUSPENDED payer (reason mandatory). A checker must approve to move the payer (and its tenants) to the terminal RETIRED state.

CHECKER step. Requires the APPROVAL_DECIDE permission and a DIFFERENT actor than the requester (else 403). Executes the requested transition (suspend/reactivate/retire) and revokes tenant sessions where applicable. Re-validates the transition against the payer's current status (409 if it drifted). Set {{lifecycleRequestId}} to the LCR… returned by the maker request.
CHECKER step. Rejects a PENDING lifecycle request (requester ≠ approver, APPROVAL_DECIDE). Payer status is unchanged.
Lifecycle maker-checker history for a payer (newest first). Access: PLATFORM_ADMIN, or the APPROVAL_VIEW / APPROVAL_DECIDE permission.

item.name: Provisioning

request.description: Save a config section. For DOMAINS_SSL, include `subdomain` (validated, unique — persisted onto the tenant) and optional `custom_domain`; other sections carry their own keys (provider, host, from_address, …). Set the `section` path var to one of DATABASE|DOMAINS_SSL|EMAIL|SMS|DATA_MIGRATION.

item.description: Tenant Provisioning (PRD step 6). Lifecycle: Onboarding (6 steps) -> Submit -> PROVISIONING (assign engineer; configure DATABASE/DOMAINS_SSL/SMS/EMAIL/DATA_MIGRATION; subdomain is set in DOMAINS_SSL) -> all sections DONE => READY_TO_ACTIVATE -> final maker-checker Approval -> auto-Activate. Approval is blocked (409) until every tenant is READY_TO_ACTIVATE.

item.name: Analytics & Metrics
item.description: Per-module analytics/metrics for dashboard & module screens (read-only aggregates). Overview = /platform/dashboard; the rest are GET <module-base>/metrics.

request.description: Directory: summary tiles (total/active/inactive) + paged, filterable list.

response.name: Example — 200 OK

request.description: List all document slots with upload/review status.
Upload/replace a document slot (multipart file). Slot → PENDING_REVIEW. ADMIN.
Fresh download URL for an uploaded slot.
Providers awaiting review + stats. APPROVER/ADMIN/SUPPORT.
Per-section review state (reviewed + open remarks), n/5, remarks.
Leave a review remark. ACTION_REQUIRED blocks activation. APPROVER/ADMIN.
Resolve an open remark. APPROVER/ADMIN.
Mark a section reviewed (rejected if it has open remarks). APPROVER/ADMIN.
Approve & activate (gated: all sections reviewed, no open remarks). APPROVER/ADMIN.
Send back to specialist → DRAFT; emails the specialist (best-effort) with open-remark count + optional note. APPROVER/ADMIN.
